macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. stocks rebound as chip shares rally; major indexes snap three-day losing streak
items.summary: The S&P 500 rose about 0.9%, the Dow gained nearly 400 points, and the Nasdaq added about 1.3% as chip stocks led a broad rebound in U.S. equities. Market commentary tied the move to improving sentiment around semiconductor names and easing near-term pressure on the major averages.

items.title: Asian markets mixed as chip strength boosts regional equities
items.summary: Asian shares were reported higher in one session, with the MSCI Asia Pacific up 0.8% and South Korea’s Kospi jumping more than 5% on strong semiconductor momentum. Another update showed the region later slipping 0.8%, led by a 3.9% drop in Japan’s Nikkei 225, underscoring volatile trading across the region.

items.title: Tesla earnings seen as a major market catalyst
items.summary: CNBC reported that Tesla shares were poised for their biggest earnings-driven move in a year ahead of the company’s quarterly report. Investors were watching the results closely for clues on demand, margins, and the outlook for the electric-vehicle sector.

items.title: Intel shares jump after new job cuts announcement
items.summary: Bloomberg Línea reported that Intel stock rose nearly 8% after the company announced additional employment reductions. The move was read by markets as a sign of deeper cost-cutting and restructuring efforts.

items.title: Fed policy expectations remain focused on holding rates steady
items.summary: Reuters reported that economists expect the Federal Reserve to keep rates unchanged for the rest of 2026 despite persistent inflation concerns. The same Reuters roundup noted traders were watching how central-bank policy interacts with tariff risks, inflation, and broader growth concerns.

items.title: Market attention turns to tariff risks as Trump prepares fresh levies
items.summary: Reuters and the Financial Times said the White House is preparing additional tariffs on dozens of countries as a temporary 10% global tariff nears expiration. Separate reports also highlighted new U.S. tariffs on some Canadian goods, adding to trade-war concerns for markets.

items.title: Oil prices rise on geopolitical tensions and supply concerns
items.summary: Yahoo Finance and CNBC reported higher crude prices, with U.S. oil reaching a five-week high as investors weighed rising U.S.–Iran tensions. The move in energy markets was also linked to inflation worries and possible spillover effects on equities and bonds.

items.title: Japanese trade data show strong export growth but lingering external pressure
items.summary: Reuters reported that Japanese exports rose 19.3% year over year in June, marking a 10th straight month of gains. Investing.com also highlighted a widening trade-balance squeeze as higher imports offset export strength.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto market adds about $63 billion as CLARITY Act progress lifts sentiment
items.summary: The crypto market gained roughly $63 billion in 24 hours, with Bitcoin trading near $66,255 as reports said the White House agreed on an ethics package to unblock the CLARITY Act. The bill would clarify SEC/CFTC jurisdiction and protect customer funds in bankruptcies, but it still needs 60 Senate votes before the Aug. 7 recess.

items.title: Bitcoin reclaims $66,000 as crypto stocks rally
items.summary: Bitcoin rose to around $66,886, its highest level in over a month, helping lift shares of crypto-linked companies such as Strategy, Coinbase, and Marathon Digital. The move came alongside broader risk-on trading and stronger equities.

items.title: More than $223 million in crypto positions wiped out in 24 hours
items.summary: Crypto markets saw heavy liquidations over the past day, with more than $223 million in positions closed and shorts accounting for the majority of losses. CoinGlass data cited in the report showed roughly $181 million in short liquidations.

items.title: Russia’s Duma approves crypto bill bringing trading into licensed financial system
items.summary: Russia’s State Duma approved a sweeping crypto bill that would bring trading under a licensed financial framework. The move is one of the most notable regulatory developments in the last day.

items.title: BIS warns stablecoins may weaken capital controls in emerging markets
items.summary: The Bank for International Settlements warned that stablecoins could undermine the effectiveness of capital controls in emerging markets. The warning adds to growing regulatory scrutiny of dollar-linked digital assets.

items.title: Polygon Ithaca hard fork set for July 29 mainnet launch
items.summary: Polygon’s Ithaca hard fork is scheduled to go live on mainnet on July 29, with the upgrade aimed at improving payment reliability. This is a significant project-development update for the network.

items.title: Movement blockchain developer files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy
items.summary: The company behind Movement, MVMT Labs, filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in Delaware, with a $1.6 million claim reported as the largest debt. The filing is a major setback for the project’s ecosystem.

items.title: Jack Dorsey launches open-source decentralized chat app Buzz on Nostr
items.summary: Jack Dorsey introduced Buzz, an open-source decentralized group chat application built on the Nostr protocol. The launch is a notable development for decentralized social and messaging tools.

items.title: Satsuma shareholders approve Bitcoin liquidation and delisting
items.summary: Shareholders of Satsuma Technology approved the sale of all Bitcoin holdings, return of capital to investors, and delisting from the London Stock Exchange. The move marks a significant unwind of a listed Bitcoin reserve strategy.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google releases cheaper Gemini models, delays flagship Pro update
items.summary: Google rolled out a trio of lower-cost Gemini AI models, including variants focused on efficiency and cybersecurity, while offering no new timing for the delayed flagship Pro model. The move underscores a broader industry push toward cheaper, more specialized models.

items.title: Microsoft deepens Mistral partnership with Europe infrastructure deal
items.summary: Microsoft said it will spend billions on Mistral’s computing infrastructure in Europe and expand distribution of the French startup’s technology through its cloud and software products. The agreement signals continued demand for regional AI infrastructure and distribution partnerships.

items.title: U.S. and China plan AI talks for September
items.summary: Five sources told Reuters that the United States and China are preparing AI discussions in September as both countries grapple with the risks posed by powerful frontier models. The talks would add to the growing diplomatic focus on AI governance and safety.

items.title: UK robotics startup Humanoid raises $152 million
items.summary: Humanoid announced a $152 million Series A round at a $1.35 billion post-money valuation. The funding reflects investor interest in robotics systems designed to augment human labor.

items.title: Anthropic faces patent lawsuit from University of Tennessee research arm
items.summary: The University of Tennessee’s research arm sued Anthropic in Delaware federal court, alleging infringement of patents related to neural networks. The case adds to the legal pressure surrounding major AI developers and model training methods.

items.title: Google DeepMind reportedly launches three mid-tier models
items.summary: Chinese-language AI news outlets reported that Google DeepMind released three mid-range models, led by Gemini 3.6 Flash, plus a Flash-Lite variant and a cybersecurity-focused model. The release is described as emphasizing lower token usage, better efficiency, and practical deployment.

items.title: Google DeepMind unveils GenCeption for multi-task visual analysis
items.summary: AIBase reported that DeepMind introduced GenCeption, a model that repurposes video generation into a visual-analysis system for tasks such as depth estimation, segmentation, and 3D pose estimation. The report says the model performs multiple vision tasks in a single forward pass.

items.title: Sakana AI launches Fugu Cyber model for cybersecurity
items.summary: AIBase reported that Sakana AI released Fugu Cyber, a defense-oriented model that posted strong benchmark results in CyberGym and CTI-REALM. The model is positioned as a new contender in AI-driven cyber defense.

items.title: Alibaba releases Qwen-Image-3.0 image model
items.summary: AIBase reported that Alibaba introduced Qwen-Image-3.0, a new image-generation foundation model with expanded text-input support and multilingual rendering. The release is presented as a major step for commercially complex image and design workflows.

items.title: AI trend: open-weight and lower-cost models continue to accelerate
items.summary: Coverage from Reuters and The Economist points to a continuing industry shift toward cheaper, open-weight, and more specialized models, especially from Chinese labs. The broader trend is reshaping competition, infrastructure spending, and government interest in sovereign AI.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Black Ops 7 Season 5 set for July 23 rollout
items.summary: Treyarch has scheduled the launch of Season 5 for Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 on July 23 across PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X/S, with rollout times listed by region. The update is one of the day’s clearest near-term game-service developments.

items.title: EA confirms Mbappé as EA Sports FC 27 Ultimate Edition cover star
items.summary: Electronic Arts has confirmed Kylian Mbappé as the cover star for the Ultimate Edition of EA Sports FC 27 and said the full reveal trailer will arrive on July 23. EA says the announcement begins a week of news for the game.

items.title: Darkest Dungeon gets first major expansion in six years
items.summary: Red Hook Studios has officially announced The Fire’s Edge, a new DLC expansion for Darkest Dungeon. The studio says it is the game’s first major expansion in six years.

items.title: Wreckreation 2 announced for PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S
items.summary: When Tides Turn has announced Wreckreation 2, a sequel to 2025’s Wreckreation. The open-world arcade racer is in development for PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X/S, but no release date has been set.

items.title: Don't Starve Together launches on mobile
items.summary: Don't Starve Together has launched on iOS and Android as a full mobile version of the game, with Playdigious handling the port. This marks a notable platform expansion for the long-running survival title.

items.title: Samurai: Ronin's Path now available on Steam
items.summary: The new feudal-Japan action game Samurai: Ronin's Path has officially launched on Steam. The release adds another new PC title to the day’s game-launch slate.

items.title: Robocop TV series gets eight-episode order at Prime Video
items.summary: Amazon Prime Video has officially greenlit a Robocop television series, according to reporting cited in gaming-news coverage. The project has been handed an eight-episode order.

items.title: Bethesda roadmap report points to Fallout 5 and more remasters
items.summary: IGN reports that Bethesda has laid out a roadmap that includes The Elder Scrolls 6, Fallout 5, a new Fallout project from Obsidian, and several remaster projects. The report is a notable industry-planning development, though it is framed as a roadmap claim rather than a formal release announcement.

items.title: Bethesda faces fallout from Microsoft layoffs amid protest at HQ
items.summary: Ars Technica reports that hundreds rallied at Bethesda HQ to protest Xbox layoffs, with union organizers calling for an end to repeated layoffs and a return to contract bargaining. The same coverage says Bethesda and id Software were reportedly hit hard by Microsoft’s reductions.

items.title: The Verge reports Fallout 5 tease after Xbox layoffs
items.summary: The Verge says Bethesda teased Fallout 5 shortly after Xbox’s mass layoffs, adding to a week of major industry turbulence. The report also notes broader gaming-news items such as Fortnite’s AI personas and Roblox’s phone-based game creation tools.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
